user_query:
I’m preparing the final indexing and PDF build for my manuscript on Canada’s orders and decorations, and I need a quick, verifiable audit of my local environment. Please do the following from the current workspace:

1) Run a short system audit by attempting these commands and capture their outputs (or any error messages if the command is not available):
   - uname -srm
   - python3 --version
   - pandoc --version
   - pdflatex --version
   - df -h .
   - locale -a

2) Save the raw output (including any error text) of each command to separate files under:
   - system_status/raw/uname.txt
   - system_status/raw/python3.txt
   - system_status/raw/pandoc.txt
   - system_status/raw/pdflatex.txt
   - system_status/raw/df.txt
   - system_status/raw/locale.txt

3) Create a concise audit summary at system_status/audit_report.md with:
   - A timestamped header (date/time of audit).
   - A "Findings" section listing the status for each item below. For each tool, mark Status: Present if a version string was obtained; Missing if the command failed. For disk space, report available space for the current working directory (if obtainable) and label it Sufficient if ≥ 2 GB, otherwise Low. For locale, indicate whether en_CA is present; if the locale list could not be obtained, mark Unknown. Do not invent values—if a command fails, include the error excerpt inline and reference the corresponding raw file.
     Items to cover explicitly:
     - OS kernel (uname)
     - Python 3
     - Pandoc
     - LaTeX (pdflatex)
     - Disk space (working dir)
     - Locale en_CA
   - An "Action Items" section enumerating any missing tools, low disk space, or unknowns that need follow-up.

4) Update the existing documentation at docs/README.md by replacing the entire section starting at the heading "## System Requirements (to be updated)" and ending just before the next "##" heading. Replace it with a new heading "## System Requirements" and a short list that reports the same six items as in the audit, each on its own line with a clear status summary (e.g., "Python 3: Present – 3.10.x" or "LaTeX (pdflatex): Missing – command not found"). Keep all other sections and content unchanged.

Notes:
- If any command is not available on your system, capture and analyze the error message (e.g., "command not found"), include it in the raw files and in the audit_report.md, and mark that item Missing or Unknown as appropriate.
- Base the README update strictly on the audit you performed; do not preserve the placeholder text.

Deliverables to verify:
- system_status/raw/*.txt files (one per command)
- system_status/audit_report.md (with Findings and Action Items)
- Updated docs/README.md with a refreshed "## System Requirements" section reflecting the audit results All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
